---
title: "LRP276: Reverse WR→LS mediation, t3 outcome (ceiling sensitivity to LRP176)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

The reverse-direction [LRP176](../lrp-rli-med-176) ($WR_{t2} \rightarrow LS_{t4}$) reads its
indirect effect on the 0–32 letter-sound scale, which is ~12% ceilinged at t4 and could
attenuate the NIE. LRP276 is the **ceiling-sensitivity companion**: the identical design with
the outcome taken at **t3** (only ~6% of children at the $LS$ ceiling), so the reverse
$WR \rightarrow LS$ indirect is read with more room in the outcome to move. Close agreement
with LRP176 means the small reverse signal is not a ceiling artefact; a materially larger t3
NIE would mean the t4 read was ceiling-attenuated.

## Design

Mediator $W_{t2}$ (Beta-Binomial on $W_{t1}$); outcome $L_{t3}$ (conditioned on $L_{t1}$,
loaded via the lagged-outcome preparation). Adjustment
$\{G, A, W_\text{pre}, W_{t1}, HS, SP\}$ — identical to [LRP176](../lrp-rli-med-176); only the
outcome wave changes (t4 → t3).

## Assumptions — read this first {#sec-assumptions}

As LRP176 / LRP76: the **t2 → t3 increment is NOT randomised** (the wait-list crosses over
after t2), and the whole decomposition is ID-2 (`GA`-confounded + treatment-induced dose
confounder), so the NIE is an adjusted association under a stated *reverse* direction, never
proof that reading drives the code. The design randomises no early word-reading shock, so the
reverse direction is intrinsically under-powered. $n \approx 53 \Rightarrow$ wide intervals.

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
